content.full_text:
Farn PolrrrcAr- PnACTTcES CorrlnrrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
December 19,2002
William Baber
Office of the Mayor
City of San Diego
202 C Street
San Diego, CA92l0l
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-02-327
Dear Mr. Baber:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Mayor Dick
Murphy regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the..Act,).1
QUESTION
May Super Bowl pins containing the mayor's name be distributed by hand under
the circumstances described in the facts below?
CONCLUSION
Since all four of the criteria in the mass mailing regulation are met, distribution of
the Super Bowl pins by hand or any means to a city employee, civic leader or volunteer at
his or her place of business is prohibited.
FACTS
To commemorate the Super Bowl that will be played in San Diego onlaruary 26,
2003, the mayor of san Diego plans to distribute up to 5,000 metal pins with the
followingtext: "MayorDickMurphy: SuperBowlxxxvll, SundayJanuary 26,2003.,,
It is common for the Super Bowl host cities to create and distribute pins as souvenirs of
the game. Public funds paid for the production of the pins that ure oificially licensed by
the National Football League.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18709-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
::,


File No. 02-327
Page No. 2
The pins will not be mailed or distributed by any means similar to mailing (i.e.,
Fed Ex, UPS, etc.). The pins will only be distributed by hand, either by the mayor or
given to city employees or volunteers, who will, in turn, distribute the pins. The intended
audience for these pins is the people involved with the Super Bowl, such as visitors to the
city, civic leaders and city employees who are helping to host the events. A civic leader,
city employee or volunteer might be handed a pin at his or her place of business.
You stated that the pins have been produced, but none have been distributed. The
pins will not be distributed until you receive our advice.
ANALYSIS
Section 89001 of the Act provides that "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing
shall be sent at public expense." Under Commission regulation 18901, copy enclosed, a
mass mailing is defined as more than 200 substantially similar tangible items delivered in
a calendar month, by any means, to recipients at their residence, place of employment,
business, or post office box. Regulation 18901(a) states:
"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is
prohibited by Section 89001 if all of the following criteria
are met:
(1) AnV item sent is delivered,by any means, to the
recipient at his or her residence, place of employment or
business, or post office box. For purposes of this
subdivision (aXl), the item delivered to the recipient must
be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record, or button, or
a written document.
(2) The itern sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other
reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared or
sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert
with the elected officer.
(3XA) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with
public moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding
$50.00 are paid with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items
are sent, in a single calendar month, excluding any item
sent in response to an unsolicited request and any item
described in subdivision (b)."


File No. 02-327
Page No. 3
If the pins are distributed by hand to a city employee at his or her place of
business, such delivery is prohibited under the mass mailing regulation. If the pins are
distributed to a city employee at his or her place of business and that employee
redistributes the pins to other employees at aplace of business, that is also a prohibited
delivery.2
The pins contain the mayor's name, public funds paid for the production of the
pins, and 5,000 pins will be distributed by January 26,2003. This is well in excess of
200 substantially similar items in a single calendar month. Therefore, all four criteria in
the regulation have been met to constitute a prohibited mass mailing.
The Commission has previously advised that brochures which are sent out in bulk
to various public or private locations for recipients to pick up on their own, and brochures
that are handed out in public areas, are not subject to the mass mailing provisions of the
Act. (Mount AdviceLetter, No. 4-95-225; Gladwell Advice Letter, No. 4-95-162;
Anaya Advice Letter,No. A-91-215; and Lavagetto Advice Letter,No. ,{-90-199.)
Therefore, if the pins are placed in a basket on a counter in either a public or
private location for recipients to pick up on their own, they are not subject to the mass
mailing provisions. Distribution of the pins at the Super Bowl is also not subject to the
mass mailing provisions since the distribution is in a public area.
This letter only provides advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Act.
Since public funds paid for production of the pins that contain the mayor's nzrme, we
suggest that you examine other areas of the law which deal specifically with the
appropriate use of public funds.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
S
Legal Division
Enclosure
JSje
I:\Adviceltrs\02-327
2 Where an item falls within the restrictions of regulation 18901, the item cannot be redishibuted
by prohibited means. (Lavagetto Advice Letter, No. 4-90-199.)

May Super Bowl pins containing the mayor's name be distributed by hand under
the circumstances described in the facts below?

the Super Bowl pins by hand or any means to a city employee, civic leader or volunteer at
his or her place of business is prohibited.

the circumstances described in the facts below?

CONCLUSION: Since all four of the criteria in the mass mailing regulation are met, distribution of
the Super Bowl pins by hand or any means to a city employee, civic leader or volunteer at
his or her place of business is prohibited.
